origin.name: Saudi Arabia
origin.post_mortem_trigger: Suspicious or unnatural deaths; courts order post-mortem with religious authorities involved
origin.key_document: Arabic death certificate, court clearance letter, and no-objection letter from local sponsor
origin.source: FCDO Saudi Arabia travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Riyadh guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation is not permitted under Saudi law. Full body repatriation is the only option. Islamic burial requirements must be considered, and embalming must be agreed with Muslim authorities.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Arabic death certificate (certified English translation required)
No-objection letter from local sponsor or employer
special_rules: A no-objection letter from the local sponsor or employer is required before the body can leave Saudi Arabia.
Islamic burial requirements must be considered. Embalming must be agreed with Muslim religious authorities.
All Arabic-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Consulates in Jeddah and Al Khobar can assist depending on where the death occurred.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Saudi Arabian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Riyadh and consulates in Jeddah and Al Khobar can register the death and advise. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Saudi Arabia to UK Repatriation: Family Guide
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Saudi Arabia. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-3 weeks. Sponsor letter, court clearance, and Arabic translation all handled. Contact us 24/7.
